2605.07280.title: Mask2Cause: Causal Discovery via Adjacency Constrained Causal Attention
2605.07280.paper_summary: Leveraging deep learning for causal discovery in time series remains challenging because existing neural methods predominantly rely on component-wise architectures that fail to capture shared system dynamics or employ decoupled post-hoc graph extraction that risks overfitting to spurious correlations. We propose $\textbf{Mask2Cause}$, an end-to-end framework that recovers the underlying causal graph directly during the forecasting forward pass. Our approach introduces an Inverted Variable Embedding and an Adjacency-Constrained Masked Attention mechanism, trained with homoscedastic or heteroscedastic objectives to capture causal influences in both mean and variance. Empirical results on diverse benchmarks, from synthetic chaotic dynamics to realistic biological simulations, demonstrate state-of-the-art causal discovery with significantly reduced parameter complexity compared to standard baselines. We further show that inferred causal structures can be used to reduce parameter count of forecasting models by more than 70% on average while maintaining predictive accuracy.
2605.07280.paper_summary_zh: 利用深度學習進行時間序列的因果發現仍然具有挑戰性，因為現有的神經方法主要依賴於組件式架構，這無法捕捉共享的系統動態，或者使用解耦的事後圖形提取，這有過度擬合虛假相關性的風險。<lb/>我們提出了 $\textbf{Mask2Cause}$，這是一個端到端的框架，能夠在預測的前向傳遞過程中直接恢復潛在的因果圖。<lb/>我們的方法引入了反向變量嵌入和一種鄰接約束的掩碼注意機制，並以同方差或異方差的目標進行訓練，以捕捉均值和方差中的因果影響。<lb/>在從合成混沌動態到現實生物模擬的多樣基準上進行的實證結果顯示，與標準基準相比，我們的因果發現達到了最先進的水平，並顯著降低了參數複雜性。<lb/>我們進一步展示了推斷的因果結構可以用來將預測模型的參數數量平均減少超過70%，同時保持預測準確性。
2605.07280.author: Omar Muhammad et.al.
2605.07280.authors: Omar Muhammad, Pasupuleti Dhruv Shivkant, Deepak N. Subramani
